text:
REFINED BRIEFING (events grouped by probative direction).
Retrieval hints: actors=['House of Representatives', 'House Ways and Means Committee'], topics=['tax cuts', 'TCJA', 'legislation', 'congress'].

## BACKGROUND (12)
- [2025-01-29] Lee M. Zeldin was sworn in as the 17th Administrator of the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) on January 29, 2025. The Senate confirmed Zeldin, a former Republican congressman from New York, by a vote of 56-42. Three Democrats, Sens. Ruben Gallego of Arizona, Mark Kelly of Arizona, and John Fetterman of Pennsylvania, joined 53 Republicans in supporting his confirmation.

During his January 16 confirmation hearing, Zeldin stated he would uphold the EPA's mission to protect human health and the environment and acknowledged the basic science of climate change. He also pledged to support EPA career staff and expressed a moral responsibility to be an environmental steward. Zeldin's confirmation aligns with President Trump's stated goal of revising environmental regulations, including a climate rule aimed at tailpipe emissions. Zeldin previously served on Trump's defense team during his first impeachment and has a 14% lifetime environmental score from the League of Conservation Voters.
- [2025-02-07] Congressional Republicans are discussing strategies to advance President Donald Trump's legislative agenda. House Republicans met at the White House on Thursday, February 6, to discuss plans. Republican senators are scheduled to attend a private dinner at Mar-a-Lago on Friday, February 7.

Disagreements exist between the House and Senate regarding legislative approaches, particularly on immigration, domestic energy production, defense, and taxes. The House seeks a single comprehensive bill utilizing budget reconciliation to bypass the Senate's 60-vote filibuster threshold. The Senate, led by Budget Chair Sen. Lindsey Graham, R-S.C., is pursuing a two-bill plan.

On Friday, February 7, Graham released a plan proposing $150 billion for national defense and $175 billion for border security, offset by spending cuts. House Republicans hold a narrow majority and face internal divisions over spending cuts to offset Trump's proposed tax cuts.
- [2025-01-03] Mike Johnson was reelected as Speaker of the House on Friday, and lawmakers were sworn into the 119th Congress in both chambers. House Republicans are scheduled to meet this weekend to finalize their legislative priorities. According to sources from a closed-door meeting, Speaker Johnson informed members that President-elect Donald Trump prefers to advance his agenda through a single, comprehensive legislative package. This proposed package would encompass tax and immigration policies. Republicans anticipate unified control in Washington once Trump assumes office later this month, though they will operate with the narrowest House majority in nearly a century.
- [2025-01-27] Donald Trump delivered a speech at the House GOP Issues Conference in Miami on January 27, 2025. Trump congratulated the reelected House Republican majority, stating they achieved their largest popular vote since 1928. He acknowledged Speaker Mike Johnson, Majority Leader Steve Scalise, Majority Whip Tom Emmer, and House Conference Chair Lisa McClain. Trump also recognized Congressional Institute CEO Kelly Strickland, Kellyanne Conway, and Hialeah Mayor Esteban Bovo, who named a boulevard after him. He characterized a recent election victory as "the most epic consequential political victory in American history" and "the most consequential election victory in the last 129 years."
- [2025-01-22] President Donald Trump took several policy actions on Wednesday, January 22, 2025. He instructed federal agency heads to place officials working on Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) programs on paid administrative leave and ordered the closure of their offices by 5 pm (22:00 GMT) that day. This action follows a previous executive order targeting federal DEI programs. Separately, 1,500 ground personnel were deployed to the southern border of Mexico, an order issued by Acting Defense Secretary Robert Salesses. Trump also gave his first television interview from the Oval Office to Fox News, discussing immigration, TikTok, and pardons for January 6 supporters. Congress passed a bill, named in honor of Laken Riley, requiring undocumented immigrants arrested for certain crimes to be jailed while awaiting trial. The bill awaits presidential signature.
- [2025-02-18] On February 18, 2025, the U.S. Senate confirmed Howard Lutnick as Commerce Secretary and advanced the nomination of Kash Patel for FBI director. Both actions occurred through party-line votes.

Separately, Secretary of State Marco Rubio, Middle East envoy Steve Witkoff and national security adviser Mike Waltz held talks with Russian officials in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, regarding the war in Ukraine. The talks followed recent phone conversations between President Trump and the leaders of Russia and Ukraine.

Domestically, President Trump signed three executive orders, including measures for government-wide cuts to federal agencies. In a Fox News interview, Trump discussed Elon Musk's role in implementing executive orders, commented on inflation, and pledged to protect Social Security, Medicare, and Medicaid from federal spending reductions.
- [2025-01-20] On January 20, 2025, President Trump issued an Executive Order titled “Unleashing American Energy.” The order directs an immediate pause of funding allocated to infrastructure projects under the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (IIJA) and the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA). Section 7 of the Executive Order mandates all agencies to immediately pause the disbursement of these funds for a 90-day review period. The review aims to ensure consistency with the Administration's policy objectives, including increasing energy exploration and production and promoting consumer choice in vehicles. Agencies are required to submit reports within 90 days with findings and recommendations. Funds will not be disbursed until these recommendations are reviewed and approved by the Director of the National Economic Council and the Office of Management and Budget. An OMB Memorandum issued on January 21, 2025, clarified that the pause applies to funds supporting programs implicated by the policy established in Section 2 of the order, specifically referring to "Green New Deal" objectives.
- [2025-01-15] The Senate Judiciary Committee held the first day of confirmation hearings for Pam Bondi, President-elect Donald Trump's pick for Attorney General. Democrats questioned Bondi's independence from Trump and her stance on the legitimacy of the 2020 election. Lawmakers also raised questions regarding Trump's FBI pick, Kash Patel, citing his past promotion of unsubstantiated claims. Republicans used their time to criticize Justice Department investigations into Trump and their Democratic colleagues. Separate confirmation hearings were also held for Marco Rubio, nominated for Secretary of State; John Ratcliffe, nominated for CIA Director; Russell Vought, nominated for Office of Management and Budget Director; Sean Duffy, nominated for Transportation Secretary; and Chris Wright, nominated for Energy Secretary. Senator Patty Murray expressed strong opposition to Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s nomination for Secretary of Health and Human Services.
- [2025-01-29] Lee M. Zeldin was sworn in as the 17th Administrator of the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) on January 29, 2025.
